// Auto-generated solution for real-time Lunar Lander controller.
// Uses a dedicated stdin reader thread so output never blocks on input.

use std::f64::consts::PI;
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};
use std::sync::{Arc, Mutex};
use std::thread;
use std::time::{Duration, Instant};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Default)]
struct State {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    vx: f64,
    vy: f64,
    angle: f64,
    angular_vel: f64,
    fuel: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct MapInfo {
    gravity: f64,
    max_thrust: f64,
    target_x: f64,
    target_y: f64,
    lz_min_x: f64,
    lz_max_x: f64,
}

fn clamp(v: f64, lo: f64, hi: f64) -> f64 {
    if v < lo {
        lo
    } else if v > hi {
        hi
    } else {
        v
    }
}

fn parse_f64(s: &str) -> Option<f64> {
    s.parse::<f64>().ok()
}

fn main() {
    let latest_state: Arc<Mutex<Option<State>>> = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));
    let map_info: Arc<Mutex<Option<MapInfo>>> = Arc::new(Mutex::new(None));

    // Reader thread owns stdin and continuously updates shared state.
    {
        let latest_state = Arc::clone(&latest_state);
        let map_info = Arc::clone(&map_info);
        thread::spawn(move || reader_loop(latest_state, map_info));
    }

    // Output loop (>= 20 Hz).
    let stdout = io::stdout();
    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(stdout.lock());

    let mut last_tick = Instant::now();
    loop {
        // Keep 50ms cadence.
        let now = Instant::now();
        let elapsed = now.duration_since(last_tick);
        if elapsed < Duration::from_millis(50) {
            thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(50) - elapsed);
        }
        last_tick = Instant::now();

        let map = map_info.lock().ok().and_then(|g| *g);
        let state = latest_state.lock().ok().and_then(|g| *g);

        let (thrust, turn) = match (map, state) {
            (Some(m), Some(s)) => control(&m, s),
            _ => (0.0, 0.0),
        };

        let _ = writeln!(out, "{:.6} {:.6}", thrust, turn);
        let _ = out.flush();
    }
}

fn reader_loop(latest_state: Arc<Mutex<Option<State>>>, map_info: Arc<Mutex<Option<MapInfo>>>) {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut reader = io::BufReader::new(stdin.lock());
    let mut line = String::new();

    // Read initial header.
    if reader.read_line(&mut line).ok().filter(|&n| n > 0).is_none() {
        return;
    }
    let mut it = line.split_whitespace();
    let width = it.next().and_then(|v| v.parse::<usize>().ok()).unwrap_or(0);
    let height = it.next().and_then(|v| v.parse::<usize>().ok()).unwrap_or(0);
    let gravity = it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(1.62);
    let max_thrust = it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(10.0);
    let _fuel0 = it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0);

    // Second line.
    line.clear();
    if reader.read_line(&mut line).ok().filter(|&n| n > 0).is_none() {
        return;
    }
    let mut it2 = line.split_whitespace();
    let _start_x = it2.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0);
    let _start_y = it2.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0);
    let target_x = it2.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0);
    let target_y = it2.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0);

    // Scan map rows for landing zone extents (cells marked 'L').
    let mut lz_min_x: i64 = i64::MAX;
    let mut lz_max_x: i64 = i64::MIN;
    let mut lz_y: i64 = -1;

    for row in 0..height {
        line.clear();
        if reader.read_line(&mut line).ok().filter(|&n| n > 0).is_none() {
            break;
        }
        let bytes = line.trim_end_matches(['\r', '\n']).as_bytes();
        // If map rows are extremely wide, only scan up to declared width.
        let scan_len = bytes.len().min(width);
        for col in 0..scan_len {
            if bytes[col] == b'L' {
                if lz_y == -1 {
                    lz_y = row as i64;
                }
                if row as i64 == lz_y {
                    lz_min_x = lz_min_x.min(col as i64);
                    lz_max_x = lz_max_x.max(col as i64);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let (lz_min_x_f, lz_max_x_f) = if lz_y >= 0 && lz_min_x <= lz_max_x {
        // [min, max+1) in meters.
        (lz_min_x as f64, (lz_max_x + 1) as f64)
    } else {
        (target_x - 2.0, target_x + 2.0)
    };

    if let Ok(mut guard) = map_info.lock() {
        *guard = Some(MapInfo {
            gravity,
            max_thrust,
            target_x,
            target_y,
            lz_min_x: lz_min_x_f,
            lz_max_x: lz_max_x_f,
        });
    }

    // Continuous state updates.
    loop {
        line.clear();
        match reader.read_line(&mut line) {
            Ok(0) | Err(_) => break,
            Ok(_) => {
                let mut it = line.split_whitespace();
                let s = State {
                    x: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                    y: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                    vx: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                    vy: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                    angle: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                    angular_vel: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                    fuel: it.next().and_then(parse_f64).unwrap_or(0.0),
                };
                if let Ok(mut guard) = latest_state.lock() {
                    *guard = Some(s);
                }
            }
        }
    }
}

fn control(map: &MapInfo, s: State) -> (f64, f64) {
    // Target x is clamped to the landing zone interior.
    let tx = clamp(map.target_x, map.lz_min_x + 0.5, map.lz_max_x - 0.5);

    // Altitude above target (positive when above, since y increases downward).
    let altitude = (map.target_y - s.y).max(-1000.0).min(1000.0);

    // Desired vertical speed (vy positive downward).
    let desired_vy = if altitude > 60.0 {
        12.0
    } else if altitude > 25.0 {
        7.0
    } else if altitude > 10.0 {
        3.5
    } else if altitude > 3.0 {
        1.2
    } else {
        0.3
    };

    // Horizontal control to bleed x error and vx.
    let ex = tx - s.x;
    let desired_ax = 0.08 * ex + (-0.35) * s.vx;

    // Convert desired horizontal accel to a desired tilt.
    // For small angles: ax ~= (thrust*max_thrust) * sin(angle).
    let a_avail = (0.85 * map.max_thrust).max(1e-3);
    let ratio = clamp(desired_ax / a_avail, -0.7, 0.7);
    let desired_angle = ratio.asin();

    // Vertical acceleration target in +down coordinates.
    let desired_ay = 0.9 * (desired_vy - s.vy);

    // thrust = (g - ay) / (max_thrust*cos(angle))
    let cos_a = s.angle.cos().abs().max(0.15);
    let mut thrust = (map.gravity - desired_ay) / (map.max_thrust * cos_a);

    // Near ground, bias to keep upright and reduce sideways drift.
    if altitude < 8.0 {
        thrust += 0.05;
    }

    thrust = clamp(thrust, 0.0, 1.0);

    // Attitude PD: command turn rate to track desired_angle.
    let mut angle_err = desired_angle - s.angle;
    while angle_err > PI {
        angle_err -= 2.0 * PI;
    }
    while angle_err < -PI {
        angle_err += 2.0 * PI;
    }

    let mut turn = 6.0 * angle_err + (-1.5) * s.angular_vel;
    turn = clamp(turn, -1.0, 1.0);

    (thrust, turn)
}
